title: Phulka Roti

subs.name: 50% atta + 50% maida for softer rotis
subs.notes: pure maida phulkas won't puff as well

subs.name: cooking oil or butter brushed on top

steps.text: Combine the atta and salt in a wide bowl. Add lukewarm water a little at a time, mixing with your fingers, until a soft but non-sticky dough forms. Knead firmly for 5 minutes until the surface is smooth.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and rest for at least 20 minutes. This relaxes the gluten so the phulkas roll thin without springing back.
Divide the rested dough into 12 equal balls. Dust the work surface lightly with flour and roll each ball into a round about 15 cm wide, keeping the thickness even so it puffs uniformly.
Heat a tawa over medium-high flame until a drop of water sizzles off instantly. Place a rolled roti on the tawa and cook for about 45 seconds until small bubbles appear on top.

Flip and cook the second side for 30 seconds until light brown specks form. Using tongs, lift the roti directly onto the open gas flame. It will puff into a ball within seconds — turn once so both sides char lightly. Remove immediately.
Stack the phulkas in a cloth-lined container. Brush lightly with ghee or butter if desired and serve at once with dal, sabzi or any gravy.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: wheat-flour → 50% atta + 50% maida for softer rotis (pure maida phulkas won't puff as well); ghee → cooking oil or butter brushed on top
